failure_mode: PowerShell chained commands fail in exec
root_cause: PowerShell does not support && or || operators for command chaining; these are bash/cmd constructs that fail silently or error under the tool runner
fix_applied: Split into multiple separate exec calls; use semicolon ; as separator within a single PS invocation

root_cause: Dollar-sign variables in inline PowerShell passed through exec are expanded or stripped by the tool runner shell layer before PS sees them
fix_applied: Avoid $var in inline PS strings passed to exec; use file-based .ps1 scripts or Python for text processing instead

failure_mode: PowerShell ForEach-Object missing $_ prefix on property access
root_cause: Inside PowerShell scriptblocks, object properties must be accessed via the automatic variable $_ (e.g. $_.Path, $_.FullName); omitting $_ causes parse errors or silent nulls
fix_applied: Always reference pipeline object as $_.PropertyName inside ForEach-Object and Where-Object scriptblocks

failure_mode: Stale browser element refs after navigation
root_cause: Browser automation element references (aria refs, cached node handles) are invalidated when the page navigates or re-renders; using stale refs causes click/interact failures
fix_applied: Use JS evaluate with fresh CSS/text selectors to find and interact with elements instead of caching refs across navigations

failure_mode: React input type action does not trigger state update
root_cause: React controls input values via synthetic events; standard browser type/fill actions set the DOM value directly but bypass React's internal state, leaving the component state stale
fix_applied: Use Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(HTMLInputElement.prototype, 'value').set to set value then dispatch both 'input' and 'change' events via evaluate to trigger React state update

failure_mode: Alicdn direct image fetch returns 403 Forbidden
root_cause: cbu01.alicdn.com enforces referer/hotlinking restrictions; direct fetch requests without a valid 1688.com referrer are rejected with 403
fix_applied: Screenshot the product page in browser instead of fetching the image URL directly; alternatively use browser evaluate to download the image blob within page context

failure_mode: Daily memory file missing when scheduled review runs
root_cause: Scheduled epistemic/nightly reviews assume the current day's memory file already exists; if the file was never initialized that day the review script errors or produces incomplete output
fix_applied: Create the day's memory file (even with minimal placeholder content) before running any scheduled review that depends on it
